# `energy/state_installed_capacity_by_source_mw`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: State installed electricity capacity, by fuel source (MW)  
**One-line**: Per-state installed electricity-generation capacity broken down by fuel source — coal, hydro, large-hydro, small-hydro, wind, solar, bio-power, oil-gas, nuclear.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_installed_capacity_by_source_mw.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_installed_capacity_by_source_mw.json)

## Definition

Per-state installed electricity-generation capacity broken down by fuel source — coal, hydro, large-hydro, small-hydro, wind, solar, bio-power, oil-gas, nuclear. The capacity is sited in the state but the electricity it generates may flow elsewhere via the national grid; read this as a 'where assets sit' map, not 'where service reaches'. Long-history (FY 2015-16 onward) companion to the CEA single-month snapshot already in the site.

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2015-04..2025-04 (11 periods)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs) (36 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 1,815

## Methodology vintage

ICED capacity-metatable rollup of CEA-published station-level capacity, harmonised across fiscal years 2015-16 → 2025-26.

## Notes

Andhra Pradesh figures before FY2014-15 include the area now in Telangana; Jammu & Kashmir before FY2019-20 includes Ladakh. Sub-fuel buckets ('large-hydro' vs 'small-hydro', 'bio-power' vs 'waste-to-energy') follow ICED's labelling and may shift year-on-year as CEA refines its source taxonomy.
